original_abstract: We report the development of an iron-catalyzed strategy for the intermolecular 1,2-addition of perfluoroalkyl iodides to alkenes and alkynes. This catalytic system exhibits broad substrate compatibility and excellent functional-group tolerance, facilitating the incorporation of diverse perfluoroalkyl groups, including trifluoromethyl (CF₃). The resulting perfluoroalkylated alkenyl and alkyl iodides serve as versatile synthetic intermediates that can be readily elaborated through subsequent cross-coupling transformations. Overall, this methodology offers a robust and efficient platform for the streamlined synthesis of complex perfluoroalkylated organic molecules.
humanized_abstract: Here we describe an iron-catalyzed method for the intermolecular 1,2-addition of perfluoroalkyl iodides to alkenes and alkynes. This approach enables the formation of a broad range of perfluoroalkylated alkenyl and alkynyl iodides, including those bearing trifluoromethyl (CF3) groups, in high yields with excellent functional group tolerance. These crucial building blocks can be further transformed via cross-coupling reactions to produce complex perfluoroalkylated molecules in a highly efficient manner.

These crucial building blocks can be further transformed via cross-coupling reactions to produce complex perfluoroalkylated molecules in a highly efficient manner.
